items: The 12V battery fluid is dilute sulfuric acid and should be handled and stored with care.
If 12V battery fluid gets into eyes or gets on hands or work clothes, wash immediately.
When charging a 12V battery, the amount of 12V battery fluid should be checked before the work is performed, as an electrode sticking out may cause the 12V battery to rupture.
Always remove the 12V battery from the vehicle when charging.
Do not allow 12V battery fluid to adhere to the vehicle as it may damage the paint or other components.

blocks.text: Ensure that the temperature of the 12V battery fluid is within the range of 15-38°C.

12V battery Check case for damage, deformation and condition of terminals, if necessary Replace the 12V battery. Prepare tester and charger. Connect 12V battery tester to 12V battery, CCA current * (at sentence-end, falling tone) indicates a confident conclusion 1/2 Load test with a load of 1.5 V for 15 seconds and measure the battery voltage immediately after the test. * CCA current: Cold Cranking Amps For M-42 capacity, CCA current is 340A Before installing the 12V battery, inspect the 12V battery terminals for corrosion. If corrosion is present, polish the terminals with sodium bicarbonate solution and rinse them clean with warm water, and polish the cable clamps with sandpaper to remove corrosion. After attaching the clamps to the terminals, apply grease to prevent corrosion.
Check the amount of 12V battery fluid in each fluid tank. If it is low, remove the cap on the top of the 12V battery and replenish distilled water to the upper limit of each cell.

items.text: If the voltage is greater than 9.6V, the 12V battery is normal. (20°C)
If the voltage is greater than 9.6V, the 12V battery is normal. (20°C)
If the voltage is in the range of 6.5-9.6 V, fast charge the battery at a charging current of 40 A for 3 minutes while observing the battery voltage.
If the voltage is in the range of 6.5-9.6 V, fast charge the battery at a charging current of 40 A for 3 minutes while observing the battery voltage.
As the voltage rises, the charging current falls, but do not increase the current value when it falls. Raising it will damage the 12V battery.
When charging, remove the cap on the top surface of the 12V battery to prevent hydrogen gas generated during charging from filling the 12V battery.
When charging, remove the cap on the top surface of the 12V battery to prevent hydrogen gas generated during charging from filling the 12V battery.
When charging, remove the cap on the top surface of the 12V battery to prevent hydrogen gas generated during charging from filling the 12V battery.
If the voltage is less than 15.5V at most, the 12V battery is normal.
If the voltage is less than 15.5V at most, the 12V battery is normal.
If the voltage exceeds 15.5 V for even a moment in 3 minutes, the 12 V battery is defective.
If the voltage exceeds 15.5 V for even a moment in 3 minutes, the 12 V battery is defective.

If the voltage is below 6.5V, charge normally for 24 hours (or until the specific gravity of the 12V battery fluid is at least 1.28) at a charge capacity of 5A. After charging, perform the load test again, and if the voltage again falls below 6.5V, the 12V battery is defective.

If the voltage is below 6.5V, charge normally for 24 hours (or until the specific gravity of the 12V battery fluid is at least 1.28) at a charge capacity of 5A. After charging, perform the load test again, and if the voltage again falls below 6.5V, the 12V battery is defective.
